dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Stockpiling some of our Adamant, eh, witchie? Thought I might ask you give it back, but we've enough for now. And I reckon you'll put it to proper use. Good stuff, ain't it?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Got to say, when we first met, I was uncertain what to make of you. Don't always trust you witchy types is all. But, if you're worthy of Aphrodite's blessing, you get the package deal.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Whew, Aphrodite, she's a real force of nature, isn't she? And just about the only thing gets me to leave the forge! Not that she minds me working all the time. We've always had an understanding, I reckon!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oi Aphrodite seems to like you quite a bit! Likes just about everybody, granted, but with you it's different. She'd never fess to it though I reckon she gets a bit lonely here, you know? Lots of acquaintances, not many friends.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Erm, wanted to say cheers for keeping Aphrodite company, being a decent listener and all. Me? I find talk more tiresome sometimes than this here forge... but not with her. Anyway! Small thing, big help.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Aphrodite's really taken to this warring stuff of late, but she is even happier now that the worst of all the fighting's done. And when she's happy, I am, too. So cheers again, witchie.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Fresh-faced Apollo got to you before me, eh? Well ain't that a surprise. He can help boost your spirit, I can boost your weapon. Tough choice, isn't it?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: They come to me sometimes and ask, where's Ares? Like I'm supposed to know just 'cause we got a brotherly connection? It's bloody war! Must be he's out there having a great time!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oi, I can't believe old Ares damn near missed the most exciting part of all of this! Showed up in time for Typhon, mind, and now he's showing up for you. Always ends up where the action is.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Run into Artemis, don't worry, she's been notified her next supply of arrows is already on its way. Chews through those things right quick, but damn if it ain't economical! She must be averaging a kill and a half per shot.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Now hold on, that another weapon you got...? Ahh, right, the Nocturnal Arms and their shape-shifting tricks. Figures! You witches like changing things about, as I'm to understand.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: How many blasted weapons do you even have by now, witchie? Long since lost track of all their forms and such, but it's a lot! You've given me so much to work with here!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Normally, it'd be ever-wise Athena who'd be first to greet you and make nice, but... she's been busy putting all my arms and armor to the test. Good news is, the testing's going well!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: All this warring, bit concerned I'll end up typecast as a weaponsmith! I make a lot of things, you know! Jewelry, fancy chairs, walking Automatons even! Though lately, it's all weapons, all the time...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oi, sorry 'bout all my Automatons up here. Heard you ran afoul of the lot! Tested 'em everywhich way... but not in this particular scenario. They give you any trouble, scrap 'em! You'll be doing me a favor.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: You might have thought I'd fix up my Automatons by now... or you're used to destroying them on sight. Well now you know how they feel! Don't reckon they feel anything, mind.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oi, that is Zorephet the Moonstone Axe, all right! Though not expected ever to lay eyes on it. Some mortals worship the labrys, you know? Here! Let's give 'em something to believe in.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Never got why some mortals prefer axes with a single blade. Why settle for one? Twice the blades, twice the benefits, I say! No downside. In fact, I've been developing a triple-bladed axe! Still needs a bit of time to bake, but, erm, anyway...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Must think I got it all up here, don't you? Well you'd be right! But let's just say you're not the only one who had to learn things the hard way, witchie. I needed to do what I do.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Nothing like a good honest fight to settle a family dispute, eh? Especially productive seeing as we can't even kill each other, as old Chronos hisself can attest. Ah well! Plenty of work for me!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Right clever of old man Chronos to recruit a bunch of bitter dead against us, ain't it? We got them killed in wars and such... reckon the payback's only fair. More chances for my handiwork to shine!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Chronos must be getting right sore from all your meetings, eh, witchie? But ain't learned a proper lesson yet. Much as it must be nice flattening him, starting to wonder what's the plan here besides that...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: I'll let you in on a secret, witchie. The best part of working a forge ain't even the weapons and armor you get, but the act of bringing 'em into being. A bit like children I reckon! Except harder to make than maintain.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Like to think I can spot the handiwork of Daedalus, best mortal craftsman ever lived; and I have spotted it! Still keeping busy nowadays, is he? I'm sure even his work leaves bits of room for improvement here and there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Ohh, this is going to be a real piece of work, since Daedalus already left his mark! I'd bless that dead old busybody bastard if I could, but I can never pin him down.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: You got the Sister Blades of Lim and Oros, huh? Delicate-looking, but damn near indestructible! Light as a feather, too. This is going to be a treat!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: I keep forgetting that you're Demeter's grand-kid! Can't stand being around her myself. Nothing personal, mind, I just can't get anything done when it's so blasted cold.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: You've not seen Dionysus, right? Probably just getting sloshed somewhere is all. Don't like when everybody's fighting... can you blame him?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oi, there's no sign of Dionysus still? Must have been quite the feast he had if he's still nursing it away! Eh, some like working and some don't. What can you do?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Heard Eris got her poor wings plucked, witchie! Next make her hand over her Rail of Adamant, would you? Shoots faster than any bow I've seen! Demands a closer look...!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Never knew your family, eh? Better off if you ask me. Though you... didn't ask me. Probably wouldn't even if you could, and I'm also keenly aware you're expecting my services not my opinions, so, erm... here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: My mum and dad, they don't get on too good. But once in a long while, there's a spark between 'em, and they're like a proper king and queen again. The envy of all gods! Lately, though, they're mostly panicking.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: There's talk up here that Chronos made off with the Fates or something like that? Well... those three have messed with all our lives so much, suppose it's only fair, ain't it?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Supposedly you're passing through the Mourning Fields along your way down there? Glad I ain't never seen a place like that. Misery's like smoke, gets in your eyes and such... hard to shake off!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Dark times of late, witchie, but what can you do? Tell you what I can do: I can sit here sweating over my forge, clanging away at armaments and such. So blasted hot and loud in here, nobody dares disturb my work. Ain't bad!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: For all the times you've been in the area, not seen my forge but once, have you, witchie? That's by design. It's like a bloody vault in here, too many valuables. Including me! Sorry I missed you, mind.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Erm, not met your dad, but... heard plenty about him. Shame, might've gotten on better with him than the rest. Likes his metals and gemstones and such as I'm to understand, so... we'd have that at least.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Your mum and dad are both all right, ain't they? This Chronos business must be pretty tough for them, if they're anything like mine. Although they don't seem anything like mine last time I checked.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Been living under the auspices of the old Witch of the Crossroads, huh? Not met her face to face. Keeps to herself, so I scarce believed it when Apollo said that she'd reached out with something important. Turns out, it was you!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: So Mum gave you her blessing, just like that? Hard won's the favor of Queen Hera. Whilst mine... reckon it's a bit easier to come by...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Got off on the wrong foot from the first, me and my mum. Can you blame her? Imagine seeing splendorous Apollo and Athena and so on, thinking, oi, I want to make me one of those! But instead, heheheh, you make me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Haven't the faintest what Hermes is up to. Where is he? Best make hisself useful, else I'm taking back the sandals and the hat! And then we'll see how blasted quick he is!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: When old Hestia said she liked you, 'twas all I needed to hear. The forge would be no good if not for her! She sparks the flame, I bend the metal, and you get the benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Now what is that you've got in hand there, let me see! Mayhap another Aspect of some sort, 'cept... augh, not seen anything like that before. I'm proper jealous now!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Ohh now what is that you've got, witchie? If I know anything of weaponcraft then that is definitely not from around here. Must still be one of those Nocturnal Arms of yours. Go on, give us a show!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: While back, made your mum and dad bit of a housewarming gift, guess you could say. Scale model of Dad's palace here, for you Underworld lot to remember us by! That Chronos better not have scrapped the thing...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: The Titan really nestled up right in your mum and dad's House, ain't that right? Now that's properly offensive... somehow more than sending all his armies after us!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: You seen that Icarus flying about? At first I took him for some giant ghostly bird, but... it's the kid of Daedalus all right! Reckon he made his latest wings hisself. He's not too bad!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: See you still got that Adamant Shard I sent over before! Just a bit of scrap, really, but... it's a shiny one, and has some decent heft. And I can use it to pinpoint you quick!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: That bit of Adamant you got from me... it's come in handy, eh? Pure as it gets, too! I hadn't thought you'd end up using it to call on me this much but... I don't mind at all.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: My mum or dad mentioned our last resort, did they? The way some of my Boons go clang, imagine that but bigger by, oh... let's just say a lot. Not keen to ever use it, mind, as then my forge would go kaboom!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: I like making armaments fit for killing mayhap one, two, let's say up to fifteen at a time? But more than that at once takes all the sport away! Mum and Dad don't get it, they just want to kill 'em all. Ares kind of gets it, though.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Keep a secret, witchie? The last resort my mum and dad asked me to build would never work. Our king and queen commissioned it to feel like they had everything in hand. I gave 'em that feeling, but... that's all I'll give 'em.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Must like to run about don't you? Me, at this point I would rather sit. And so I do! Won't find a better set of legs than this anyplace besides on Aphrodite herself! Yet even still.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: All right, that's called Revaal, I think, that skull you got? Ain't exactly my mental picture of a weapon, liable to give me night terrors in fact, but... then I'll be the first to say, whatever works!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: So, you're a witch or something, I'm to understand? Then what do you need me for? Mayhap that fancy magick of yours ain't all it's cracked up to be! My work, witchie, is reliable.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Those mortals, they sure got it rough of late. Sometimes I think there's just so blasted many of them now. More suffering and such! Eh, reckon that's life! Don't get any say in being born, then do what we can.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oi what I said before, 'bout more mortals begetting more suffering and the like? Aphrodite, she reminded me there's more to it than that. And maybe she's right. It's just, I reckon the bad in things is easier to spot...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Where'd you get off to, witchie? The Underworld's gutter from the looks of it, all cold and wet... piss-poor working conditions, that much is clear.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: You've met most of the lot up here by now. So now you know how come I'm cooped up in this forge. The work needs doing, not to mention it's less tiring than being with the family all the time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oi, you made it all the way up to the mountain earlier? I'd give you a tour of the old forge next time, it's just... it's sealed up. Damn near impossible to find! Security's sake.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Got a funny feeling something different's going on, as if... eh, I don't even know! Strange forces are afoot, witchie. Right stranger than the forces gathered here!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oi how'd you get there so damn quick, witchie? Mayhap some of your witchie stuff, but I've got something more reliable here just in case.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Heard you talked to mum and dad, face to face! Not even I get to do that much. Must have been right nervous meeting 'em. Or mayhap not considering what you did to get there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Not letting you back in the Palace, eh? Don't take it personal, it's just our bloody pride! Mum and dad aren't keen on taking guests till everything is perfect, but... it never really is.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: For all these scuffles with Prometheus, reminds me there was once this lass who, after right mean circumstances, accidentally cursed her kind forevermore. Long story long ago, but... I always thought mayhap she cursed us, too.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: No telling how many cousins I got, but you're another, eh? You're getting special treatment only 'cause my mum and dad, they asked me real nice. Don't mind the extra work!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Ran into no-eyed Polyphemus, so I hear? Mean bastard, big, all meat and muscle. Could've used that one at the old forge. Ah well! Hope he doesn't try to chew you up or nothing!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Ahh, Uncle Poseidon's been of some assistance, then? Heh! Reckon it's more welcome than him stomping uninvited in my forge, all sopping wet, filling the place with steam. Psh. What a kidder!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: This lot up here act like they all was born with all the talent in the world! Mayhap Apollo and Athena, sure, and Artemis, and Hermes... Aphrodite definitely, too, but, erm... point is! Most of us, we got to work at things before we're any good.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: So we've got Titan problems real close to home! Blasted shame about Prometheus, but then... I reckon if Dad chained me up and had an Eagle chew my liver everyday, I'd be mad too.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't want to get involved in all the ugly business with Prometheus and Dad. They won't be sipping Ambrosia anytime soon... but at least nobody's getting livers eaten now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, if it ain't my finest client! Reckon we've reached the stage of our relationship where I needn't be conversing much, and can stick to what I do best. Keep sticking, too, witchie!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't know if it's your silver gear or what, but for a bit there I thought I was staring at the Moon... don't much get to see the Moon in my line of work.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Something's been going on out there, the others saying it's a proper nasty night! Makes no difference in my forge, but... sounds like you are really in for it, witchie.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well everybody's all worked up tonight! I've no idea why. Mayhap some witchie stuff only you know about? 'Cause even my Automatons have been behaving bad... worse than usual I mean!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Say, where'd you get that silver armor you've got on? Much finer work than first I thought. Not too much coverage, mind, but you seem more the sort that likes to freely move about. Always a trade-off to be made!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: So that's the Staff of Dusk, is it? Descura, that's her name! Forged who-only-knows-how-deep within the Earth under the watchful eye of Night... something like that.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Am I mistaken, witchie... or are you bearing Xinth the Black bloody Coat? Wearing it, I should say! Heard how it's put together but I've never seen it for myself! Let's see what it can do.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Welcome to the fine city of Ephyra, witchie! Most important thing to know is, whatever happens to you there, I cannot be held liable. I work weapons, not miracles...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Ain't you not supposed to be able to breathe up here or something? Well, what can you do but push on through, is what I say! Ain't always easy but we manage or we try.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Don't know where all these Satyrs, Harpies, and whatnot are getting their supplies. Mayhap looting the ones you dealt with just the night before? Either way, looks like old-world Cyclopean design to me. It's solid work!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oi! What are you doing on this side, you lose your way? Thought you had business in the Underworld, not here. Mayhap I'm just a bit out of the loop is all... you need this stuff or not?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: I heard the latest, witchie, that you're headed for our mountain tippy-top! Just don't expect warm welcomes when you get here, as we are a... a bit preoccupied right now?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't know what's been with all the rumbling of late, but it ain't Poseidon, and it ain't my forge. Probably something bad! But, I ain't sweating it till I know what it is.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Ran into mighty Talos, didn't you? Big man all made of bronze. Swore up and down he'd be my best invention yet, but... still got a few kinks need sorting out. Don't bother going easy on him, got to take him apart anyhow...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: I tried to make it so that Talos would begin to learn as he encountered different combat situations and the like, but... I ain't seen much evidence that's doing any good. He's not been too much of a pain has he?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Wonder what Chronos did to Tartarus? Was quite the architectural feat under your mum and dad! But the Titan hasn't got any taste. Probably turned everything to gold...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: See all those Chronos ships trudging this way, witchie? Most of 'em already sunk before, however many times. They and their dead crews keep coming back to haunt us! Now that's some dedicated service, eh?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Know what I learned in all this, witchie? No time like the present. Whatever happened in the distant past ain't my fault! Might be my problem though! As for what happens in the future, ach, I'll handle it then. Well, back to work!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hold on, that's Ygnium you've got! The Flames of the Crossroads Witch! What, has the student become the master or somesuch, or is she just getting you to do all the dirty work?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: You seen the size of the bloody monster been clambering up our mountainside, witchie? I haven't, thankfully, but... hearing that he's pretty big in size! Typhon hisself! I scarce can keep a steady hand with him about!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oi, where'd that great big monster out there go? The mountain's rumbling's all settled down and now my clanging's just about the worst of it. You got him then? Ach, I didn't even get to have a look.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: What I really want to know is, how'd you blast old Typhon into smithereens? Don't have much here could've done something like that, least not without taking the summit too. You got to let me in on it sometime.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Wait... has Typhon been destroyed or no? Not seen much sign of monsters, which ain't known for subtlety. But then I heard my dad talking about how you've been dealing with their remnants here. Mayhap they're real small!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Time for more spelunking, eh, witchie? Going damn near to the bottom of the bloody earth! Must be good rocks and metals and such there. Your dad got all the hidden gems!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Feel any tremors, they are probably Uncle Poseidon shaking up the place. Though there are times it's me! When Earth herself gets angry with the state of things, I give 'er a good outlet to let off a little steam.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oi, I take back everything I said about your weapon first we met. In my defense, ain't often I see workmanship superior to mine! Though, don't go telling anyone I said as much...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Wracked my brain about those fancy silver weapons of yours, trying to sort out who exactly could have made such things... or when? They're the Nocturnal Arms, ain't they? Meant only as a last resort! But here we are, I reckon.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: I'll be the first to say one shouldn't judge by looks, but... I just didn't figure you to be so keen on proper weapon maintenance, 'cause yours are strong as hell! And making them shine makes me shine.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: While back, your brother came to us for help, what's his name... Zagreus? I wasn't having it. This family's problems would disappear overnight if we could just stop getting in each other's business! Felt bad sitting it out, though...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: You and Zagreus been getting on? Known my brother all my life... must be something that you're only just getting to know him now! Try not to fight too much. Heh, but then again, that's what brothers are for!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Reckon my dad'll be of help in all this! Lord Zeus, he loves to help! Especially hisself... lot on the line right now, and you don't get to be the king of Olympus being coy.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Nobody wants you playing favorites, witchie, so we've got this method of delivering you unmarked Boons where you'll just have to take what you can get. Surprise!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Mayhap expecting someone else? Or just as likely not expecting anybody in particular, 'cause a blessing is a blessing, ain't that right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: We used to get our Boons to you this way all secret-like, but now we're thinking mayhap there's just something to you getting a surprise! Who doesn't like those? Besides me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't sweat it, you'll be as good as new here in a bit.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oi witchie, let me see about that hunk of metal you got there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: You still alive, witchie? Here, this ought to keep it that way.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Just clanking away as always, though let's do some clanking now on your behalf.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Reckon you're looking for another tune-up, no problem at all.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: No charge per usual, I'll just throw it on tab and some point Dad'll pick it up.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: So you got a bit of metal I could effortlessly bend to my will or something?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: I just had the strongest urge to bang on solid metal for a bit.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oi, you're about due for another servicing appointment on that thing.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: You got a dangerous line of work, witchie. Though I can get you some protection here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: How's it going and the like, all good? All good, that's great, now here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Eugh, it's been an awful lot of late! Don't sweat it, though. Always got room for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Ehh, think I got just the thing to help you with the killing-and-not-dying here, witchie.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: You know, the more I work those weapons, the more intricacies I can find...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: I don't normally just give away my services, but it ain't normally of late.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Say, what's that you got there, witchie, a nice weapon of some sort?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Ehh, fancy meeting you again and all, now let me get to work.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Only so much I can do when you're out and about with that walking stick, you know.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Ehh, it's that staff again... well, mayhap I can do something about the knots at least.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Fine couple of knives you got there, witchie. Though let's make 'em proper vicious here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hold up those flashy blades of yours for me and I'll have 'em sharper than ever.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Ahh, brought my favorite out to play, did you? Just needs a tune-up and you'll be all set.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Who needs witchery when you can go on brandishing an axe like that?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Bringing a couple flaming sticks to a fight? Well, whatever works, witchie.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Ehh, turns out torches ain't just for lighting your way! Let's see 'em here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: All sorts of silver I can work with in that skull of yours, so put 'er up!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Ohh, right sinister choice of weapon you got there, witchie.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Soon as I'm through, you go knock some sense into some wretches for me, would you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Fine suit you got there, witchie! Though, reckon we could spice it up a bit!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Your suit seems in fine working order, though a tune-up couldn't hurt.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: I always said a bit of evening work is good for you, witchie.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: You work for the family, you're entitled to the family protection plan.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oi, you got something from Daedalus already? Well I can work with that.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Come on up, the weather's fine! So I hear. Been in my forge a while now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Apologies if my Automatons make any trouble for you whilst you're here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm not the one out there taking hits. Least I can do is soften 'em for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Been keeping busy underground? I can relate better than anybody here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: You've been on a proper rampage, witchie! So let's see you keep it up.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: How's things there in the Underworld, all right? Here they're not bad.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oi there's my favorite Moonstone Axe! Go cleave something in two for me, witchie.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Using the classic staff tonight? The kind I make can't shoot, but... yours is giving me ideas.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Brought out those blades for yet another go! I really like the curvy one!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Never thought of using torches for a weapon, but you really make it work!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: That skull is not exactly in my area of weapons expertise, but I can tell it works!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: I always like it when you use Xinth the Black Coat. Makes everything go boom!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What, is he going to fit me for a suit of armor? In the name of Hades! Olympus, I accept this message!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oi, you that new cousin of mine? They told me you needed a weapon or something. Ach, you already got one! Right lousy one, can spot that piss-poor workmanship from here. Though, I could tune it up...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Lord Hephaestus! Your services must be in very high demand. I'm grateful, and doubtless so are the Nocturnal Arms.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Look I don't work for tips. Though ours ain't an ordinary arrangement, and ain't often lately I get new regular clients! So, here. Call it a trade.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: For you, mighty Hephaestus! Though, you may not have occasion for such offerings, working a hot forge every night and day...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oi, this is just a service job, no need to get all cordial and the like. Nice gesture from you, though. So cheers and all.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Great Hephaestus! Your work is beyond compare, and I'm grateful for your candor with me as well. Please, take this offering.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Anytime I get an offering it's 'cause somebody wants something from me. You're already getting premium service, but hey, it's your Nectar! Well, mine now I reckon.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Lord Hephaestus, you're so much more than your ingenious craft. I never could have expected you to be so generous to me, and I'm most grateful for it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oi witchie, is it hot in here, or are you embarrassing me to no end? Just let me work the blasted forge, no need to make a big deal.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: An offering to my great cousin Lord Hephaestus, master of the forge! Such skill and artistry runs in our family, and so much of it with you. And you tolerate such intense heat without so much as a complaint!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: It does get awful hot here in my forge. Suppose a Nectar ought to cool me off! Not that I ought be drinking at a job like this, but hey! No one's watching, no one needs to know.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: If only we could have met when we weren't in the midst of a war, Lord Hephaestus, but at least I had the opportunity to meet you at all. Your dedication to your craft is an inspiration! I strive to be as skilled at mine.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oi, cheers! Don't know you all too well, and don't like drawing up conclusions till I'm very sure of something, but... you seem a decent enough sort. Much more than can be said for certain members of the family, ain't it?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Hear me, Lord Hephaestus, for this offering of Ambrosia is for you, the hardest-working blacksmith on Olympus or below! Through everything that's happened, you've remained steadfast and calm, and helped me do the same.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: You think me calm, hah! Inside, witchie, I'm screaming half the time. I just get to pound this metal against that to let the anger vent. Other half the time, I'm feeling pretty good, knowing we found ourselves a decent sort in you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...You wouldn't know it, witchie, but I'm mad this evening. Nothing you did, mind! Fortunately, when I'm mad, I do good work. My best damn work if I say so myself...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Can't guarantee a weapon worthy of the gods is going to be enough for you to see this through. But I can guarantee you're getting the very best.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: That Gold you used to pay for this... hoh, it's shiny-looking, sure, but... blasted soft as metal goes! Cannot make halfway decent armaments with it, I've tried!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Your Boatman over there? He's one of my best customers, you know! Shows up reliably, knows how to keep his mouth shut, all business, no nonsense.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Normally my work fetches a hefty price, but... you're entitled to the family discount! Though Charon there does take a service fee, you understand.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Keep getting shipments of those golden Chronos coins! I melt 'em down, and dump the goop into the Earth whence it came... so Chronos can make more!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Having to pay for blessings strikes me as a bit crass! The favor of the gods only for the deepest pockets? Oi! You don't even have pockets.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: You look like you've been beaten like a chunk of ore! Just keep it together long enough to appreciate my handiwork.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Eugh, you're in shambles there, witchie. Mayhap this'll be too little too late! Could've come in handy earlier.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, you look plain awful right now, and that's coming from me! Though hey, you get a free weapon service with the, erm... unwelcome remark.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: So you took some hits along the way, so what? You're an extension of your weapon, witchie! And a decent one can take a lot of punishment.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: All right, we're squared away for now, and break time's over. Back to work!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: That's what I like to call hammering out the details. Leave 'em to me next time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Eh! Fine, consider me appeased. But don't incur my wrath again lest I— I don't even know.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Was as close as I am liable to get to blowing up. Still working at it, mind!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Now that you've seen some samples of my work, perhaps you'd like some for yourself?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Some work you did! Though, looks like you could use some patching up.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Eh, no use staying mad over nothing! Wasn't all that mad, mind.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Been working on my anger issues. Can't blow my top like some of the others here...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: All right, I'd say we're settled up! We can continue bickering another time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hrm, that there was not the most productive thing I did all day, but eh... this ought to make up.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Congratulations, witchie, you survived. And here is what you won.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't take these fights too personal, it happens up here all the bloody time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: All right, I count you settled up after all that. Time to get back to work!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't let anybody know, but... I held off back there. 'Cause I ain't really mad.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: I can appreciate an armament of damn near any shape or size, but... ones shaped like that Moonstone Axe you got might be my favorite were I forced to choose at weaponpoint. Let's see what you can do with it, witchie!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Everybody here is going off about the Fates deciding to show up unceremonious, to which I say: so what? Nobody knows what they're doing, them least of all. Ach, mayhap I'm just jealous they'll be taking time off work...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't know what's such a big a deal with the Three Fates going on about a distant time. You're telling me that in the span of, I don't know, twenty-something mortal generations, things might change? Ach you don't say!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: You bloody did it, then? Whatever it is, everybody here has got their own idea, but... I reckon Typhon's gone and Chronos is touched in the head, so... all in all, not bad.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Got to keep on climbing up here, I'm to understand? Bit of insurance against any other Typhon-like events... and you and Chronos and the lot of us are gonna see to that? Well all right!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: So Grandpa Chronos keeps on lending us a hand! To deal with what he did to us, but still! I reckoned he'd be back to his old self by now, but... mayhap you scrambled him enough that it'll stick. Heh... think you could do that to my mum?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Thought I was due to catch a break once all the fighting and whatnot had simmered down, but no! The fighting must go on, which come to think is no surprise, I mean, it's us! We find a way!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Whoever messes with me, they get pounded flat. No exceptions! Not even for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: I don't stick up for myself, I'll never hear the end of it. So best ready for the sparks to fly a bit.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Eh, much rather keep on hammering this metal over here, but... I can switch over to you instead.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Push me around, I'm liable to push right back. And I push harder than you might expect...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Not going to blow my top... but neither will I take this lying down. Family requirements!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: You brought this on yourself, witchie! And by this, I mean my favorite hammer here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Eh, don't care for having to retaliate like this, but... it's all part of the deal, you understand.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: No denying Aphrodite was the fairer choice, but still! Can't let this go unchecked.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: You want to see my handiwork, then fine! Might get a wee bit noisy, though.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Next I offer you protection, witchie? You'd be wise to take me up on it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: You'd go warring with my brother over me? Where do you think he gets his gear?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: I'd pick Aphrodite too but... she's expecting me to fight for her, you understand.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: My dad's not used to anybody standing up to him, but we can get him there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Mayhap I'll make me mum real proud if I act spiteful without proper cause!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Next time, make Ares mad not me. He's the one wants an excuse to fight!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Had an opening come up in my schedule, so I figured why not get your gear back to better-than-new condition right from the start?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oi, wanted to catch you right on your way out! Got a special this evening: free tune-ups for secret silver weapons, if you got one there?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Fine evening to you, witchie! My work ain't quick, but sometimes I can be. And here I thought, why don't I join her from the get-go, eh? Can't hurt!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Where do you think you're off to, eh? Best not head out against impossible odds whilst unprepared, so here! Let's get you suited up right quick.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Off to pay us all a visit then, or try? Reckon you're liable to get into a tussle or two on the way, but... I can offer a complete protection plan against that sort of thing!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: It's good to have you back up top, witchie, because things on the mountain are a little touchy still. Or are we keeping it that way on purpose? Either way, doesn't bother me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Off to tell your mum and dad the latest after paying us a visit here? Well, may you clobber anybody in your warpath, witchie! 'Specially if it's some other bad Chronos or whatever it is you're doing down there now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Ohh, there you are! We'd lost you for a bit! Must have gone so deep, we couldn't spy on you no more. Well, you've still got your arms and legs! Must have turned out all right.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Am I to understand you gave a certain Titan a right thorough walloping of late? Mayhap I helped in my own way? Not like you took down Chronos with your bare hands, eh? Point is, good job, witchie!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: No point in wallowing, witchie! Whatever's going on out there, you got to love what you do, simple as that. Though don't take it from me! Got the authority right here!

dialogueLines.speaker: AphroditeUpgrade
dialogueLines.text: Why, thank you, dear! We each have so much love to give. And the beauty of it is, the more we give, the more we have! To think that such a precious thing can have a limitless supply!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: If I'm supposed to be so good with my hammer, then how come I've yet to make a suit of armor worthy of Aphrodite, eh? Least I got her to pick up a shield. Oi Aphy, you trying something on, or what?

dialogueLines.speaker: AphroditeUpgrade
dialogueLines.text: Oh Hephaestus, how you worry about me! But I don't like to be weighed down, and would much rather keep my options open than commit to any of these lovely specimens. Why not try our Underworld companion on for size?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oi, witchie, was just chatting with Apollo how not all of us are gifted with good looks, and silver tongue, and all. Got to work at something to be good at it! Like he couldn't imagine!

dialogueLines.speaker: ApolloUpgrade
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I can more than imagine, Brother. Part of being talented is making things look easy. Take you and blacksmithing! For me it's quite like that, but with a bunch of other things!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: You can spot the family resemblance between Ares and myself, can you, witchie? Mayhap he got the looks, but I got all the personality, didn't I, Brother?

dialogueLines.speaker: AresUpgrade
dialogueLines.text: I grant you that we lack the same behavior patterns, Hephaestus. But if there is to be such competition between us, then perhaps Aphrodite herself could be the judge? If you are open to the possibility, of course.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Your grandnan and I, we don't usually get talking very much. But now we got something in common, witchie: you! I say you've got things well under control, but... I can't fool the likes of Lady Demeter!

dialogueLines.speaker: DemeterUpgrade
dialogueLines.text: Not one of us has got things well under control, Granddaughter. So pay what Hephaestus said no heed! Instead use our strength combined... and Fates willing, restore for us some sense of normalcy.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: My Mum the Queen could not believe that you and I had been conversing lately, witchie. So I had to prove it to her here, you understand. Though seems she wants to be conversing in my stead...

dialogueLines.speaker: HeraUpgrade
dialogueLines.text: My son Hephaestus need only prove he can exude the confidence befitting a god, rather than whatever it is that he exudes instead. He's made himself useful at least, yes?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Witchie, I am meeting right now with none other than the Queen herself! And she is saying she would like a word. And I am saying yes Mum, anything you say, go right ahead.

dialogueLines.speaker: HeraUpgrade
dialogueLines.text: You see, my dear? One of the wondrous things about having some children of your own is seeing how obsequious they can become! Go on, Hephaestus. Give her our best.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: The mortals, they like giving thanks to us, but what are we supposed to do, you know? Well, I just do the same. Give thanks to Lady Hestia, at least! For keeping my forge burning hot. She even visits me now and again!

dialogueLines.speaker: HestiaUpgrade
dialogueLines.text: Well, that's because I like it here, my lad! In spite of all the clanging and whatnot, it's peaceful, more or less. And you're not such bad company to keep. Though, why don't we show your cousin what we can cobble up together?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Proud family, this one! Uncle Poseidon here forgoes full armor on account that he's too quick and too thick-headed, erm... thick-skinned, I mean to say. Ain't that the case, Uncle?

dialogueLines.speaker: PoseidonUpgrade
dialogueLines.text: Come, Nephew, you know how much I value your craft! Even if I'd not be caught dead wearing much of it myself! It's just, only a coward goes to battle clad in plating head-to-toe; a coward far more likely to withstand his wounds, but still!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hephaestus_01
dialogueLines.text: Always grand whenever our Lord Zeus decides to drop by unannounced, and watch over my shoulder whilst I work my forge! Even likes to point where my hammer ought to strike. Well, Dad, where to next?

dialogueLines.speaker: ZeusUpgrade
dialogueLines.text: Need I remind you, my good son, that this is not, in fact, your forge, but a facility that all of us may use? And I would use it now to benefit your distant cousin over there! Erm, show me how to use this thing again...
